name: Removal Type Hierarchy
rationale: Oxford Principles establish that carbon removal with durable storage is more climate-valuable than avoidance. Engineered removal with geological storage represents the highest-integrity intervention.
data_source: Methodology category (mapped from registry methodology ID)

bands.label: Engineered removal + geological storage
bands.criteria: Atmospheric CO2 capture; geological injection with >10,000 yr durability

bands.label: Engineered removal + durable storage
bands.criteria: Atmospheric CO2 capture; conversion to stable mineral/chemical form with >1,000 yr durability

bands.label: Nature-based removal + long storage commitment
bands.criteria: Biogenic CO2 removal with contractual/legal permanence mechanisms (>100 yr)

bands.label: Nature-based removal + moderate storage
bands.criteria: Biogenic removal with standard registry permanence mechanisms (buffer pools)

bands.label: Avoidance with high certainty
bands.criteria: Prevents emissions that would otherwise certainly occur; direct measurement possible

bands.label: Avoidance with moderate certainty
bands.criteria: Displaces emissions based on baseline calculations with reasonable counterfactual

bands.label: Avoidance with baseline uncertainty
bands.criteria: Credit depends heavily on contested counterfactual baseline

bands.label: Questionable intervention
bands.criteria: Project type has been shown to lack statistically significant climate benefit
examples: Wind power crediting in liberalized markets

notes: Hybrid projects score based on the primary intervention type.
Mapping from registry methodology ID to category is maintained in data/mappings/methodology-to-category.json.
